title: Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs discusses infrastructure patterns for managing non-deterministic AI agents in production. The talk covers orchestration, observability, retries, safety guardrails, and reliability mechanisms needed to bridge the gap between deterministic microservice platforms and long-running autonomous AI systems.
writeup: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs outlines infrastructure patterns for running non-deterministic AI agents in production, focusing on the gap between deterministic microservice platforms and long-running autonomous systems. The talk addresses orchestration, observability, retry mechanisms, and safety guardrails, along with reliability concerns such as tenant isolation, elastic compute, and control plane design. The core takeaway: agent reliability depends on infrastructure that handles error states and inference variability rather than assuming deterministic behavior.
